probe.observations: ROUND 10 IS ANOTHER ZERO-FAIL SWEEP, and the weight-4 redstone family finally cashes: every acceptance item I could reach for lever/wire/lamp/door verified first-try, with the exact decay arithmetic (15-power, -1/step) readable straight from block ids in a player-built 16-wire causeway. The lamp inheriting the torch registry is the standout integration: one toggle flips light pools, remeshes, AND the hostile-spawn gate ('light' reject beside a lit lamp at midnight, 'ok' + real zombie after toggling off), and a lit lamp survives save/reload still registered (0.857 one block away post-load). Propagation is the documented instant-on-edit semantics: every circuit read in this report was taken synchronously in the same eval as the interact() with zero frames driven between. PLAY-FEEL NOTES: (1) The kiting skeleton genuinely evades static aim - at 9 blocks my aim-at-current-position arrows whiffed 4 straight while it strafed; closing to 5 blocks landed 3/3. That is emergent lead-the-target gameplay from dumb parts, and it makes the bow feel earned rather than hitscan. (2) The sheep chain converts cleanly in play: noon passive mix spawned 3 sheep naturally, 4 bare-hand hits, wool drop, pickup, bed craft, sleep, respawn-at-bed - the whole nine-rounds-dark chain is now a 90-second loop. (3) Corpse-run stakes compose with beds exactly right: die 40 blocks out, respawn AT YOUR BED hp 20, run back, 17 full stacks waiting (still there at 90s; ordinary Q-drop despawned on schedule). CAVEATS FOR JUDGES: (a) headless tab is rAF-starved (native rAF fires roughly never until a screenshot forces a BeginFrame; I hijacked requestAnimationFrame and drove ~1500 synthetic frames per page load) - HUD FPS 0-18 in screenshots is an artifact, perf().build avg 2.8ms/chunk (max 65ms first-gen) is the honest number; an interactive judge should confirm foreground 60fps. (b) One bare-armor hp step read -2 instead of -3: full-hunger regen ticked +1 between my 0.8s samples - the underlying hits are -3 (three other steps exact). (c) Door powered-behavior check was inconclusive by my own error (my wire run to the door had a diagonal gap so it never powered); header honestly documents doors as manual-only this slice and there is no door-power code path - door ids did stay 31/32 through adjacent lever toggles. (d) place() with zero items of the selected type silently no-ops (cost me the branch lamp; branch POWER still proven by wire ids 28/28 and the cut test). (e) Shore columns near spawn reject spawnAt as 'water' - honest vocab, but gating tests need inland cells. (f) Skeleton stick drop 1-of-2 kills this round vs 50% nominal - fine. Heap 47MB after the full session incl. reload; console clean except the known three.js r160 deprecation; __vox.errors [] across ~25 evals, two deaths, and a reload on both page instances. Feel: lever-click to lamp-light to zombie-free porch is a real survival-base loop now; beds+corpse-run+bow turn night from a damage tax into tactics.

top_gaps.title: Redstone depth slice 2: powered doors + an inverter source + pressure plate/button
top_gaps.detail: The family still holds ~2.0 of its 4 weights and is the largest remaining named pool on the board. The shipped slice is power DISTRIBUTION, not logic: levers are the only source and nothing inverts, so no gate is constructible. Add a redstone-torch-analog NOT source, make doors react to adjacent power (closing the probe's flagged hole - the id machinery 31-34 already swaps atomically), and a pressure plate for input variety. The proven 200-cell BFS substrate carries it, but an inverter in an instant-propagation model can oscillate - compute a bounded fixpoint (cap iterations, settle to the last stable state) and re-run the causeway/branch/persistence probes plus the save round-trip.

top_gaps.title: Villages third weight: an inhabitant and a loot chest (FOURTH round stalled at ~2.3/3)
top_gaps.detail: grep is still clean for chest/villager/loot while everything around the hamlets got finished. A villager-flavored passive on the proven MOBDEF substrate leashed to the hamlet center, plus a take-only chest reusing the furnace slot machinery with 2-3 seeded items, converts the lit night destination into a payoff. Keep writes inside the single-chunk footprint; re-verify byType 7/109/22 and the (488,440) site bit-identical.

top_gaps.title: Creeper: third hostile archetype with block-destroying explosion
top_gaps.detail: Combat is full on depth but thin on archetypes (melee chaser + kiting archer), and now that players build redstone bases, doors, and beds, a threat that destroys blocks multiplies every system just landed - the bed-destroyed respawn fallback and corpse-run stakes are already wired to punish it. Hiss-fuse on proximity, bounded blast radius via setB+touched (which auto-depowers circuits and pops torches/wires correctly already, line 926). Explosions touch terrain edits and remesh: bound the radius, never break bedrock, and re-run determinism/save sweeps.

top_gaps.title: Arrow item + ammo economy (convert the bow's flagged infinite-ammo substitution)
top_gaps.detail: The bow is verified but fires infinite arrows; skeletons drop sticks as a bone substitute and no arrow item exists. Add arrow as a craftable item (stick + coal-as-flint analog) and a skeleton arrow drop, consume one per shot with the existing stack machinery, keep the 8-cap pool. Small, bounded, and it converts a flagged hole in an already-credited feature into clean MC-direction parity.

top_gaps.title: Boats or minecart+rails: first vehicle
top_gaps.detail: Charter-named remaining work with no rubric family weight of its own - gains flow through 65/80-band anchor interpolation, so yield is lower than the named pools. A boat on the existing water/swim physics (board via interact, WASD steers, exits on land) is the cheaper of the two; minecarts need a rail block family plus path-following. New entity-player coupling is the risk: keep it out of the save format first slice and gate behind the swim/knockback regressions.

top_gaps.title: Pure probe conversions: open the in-world 3x3 table + brush a cactus (tenth-round debt, zero code)
top_gaps.detail: Two coded-but-dark legs ten rounds owed: right-click the gen-placed hamlet crafting table and screenshot the 3x3 panel (it gates the crafting family's last ~0.3 weight - every grid proof flows through matchGrid), and walk into a cactus bare then armored to verify contact damage on a non-mob source. Hardens two credited families' weakest evidence before a hostile seat finds the seam.

top_gaps.title: Chunk data eviction with edit-replay safety (tenth-round honesty debt)
top_gaps.detail: chunks Map still grows ~16KB per visited chunk forever (zero chunks.delete); regeneration is deterministic through three generator changes and editB replay already restores edits - now also carrying redstone block-state, which makes the replay path MORE load-bearing. LRU-evict beyond ~3x render distance mirroring dropMesh, then verify torchR re-registration (torches AND lit lamps, line 770) and a powered circuit's state after a far round trip. Mostly the perf seat's credit but it protects my domain's persistence story.

top_gaps.title: Light family's last sliver: flood-fill or full-block single walls
top_gaps.detail: The verified 1-solid=x0.25 LOS model now also serves lamps, but light still dies around corners where MC wraps and leaks 25% through single walls where MC blocks. A bounded per-chunk BFS through air seeded from torchR would finish the weight-3 family's last ~0.3. It touches the mesh hot path that has been stable for six rounds and now carries lamp flips - isolate the lane, re-run the 837-cell field sweep, the lamp A/B, and both spawn-gate regressions.

judges.summary: Round 10 is the systems round of the marathon: the weight-4 redstone family went from nine rounds at zero to ~2.0/4 with every acceptance item verified first-try (exact 15-power/-1-step decay read from block ids, branch and cut-depower semantics, save persistence, and the standout lamp-torchR integration where one lever toggle flips light pools, remesh, and the hostile spawn gate), while the nine-rounds-dark sheep chain converted into a 90-second verified loop that pays off in beds, bed-respawn composes with corpse-run stakes into real night tactics, and the bow closes the ranged asymmetry with combat arithmetic that slots exactly into the attack table. Against the round-9 merge of 44.4 - a build with no redstone, no beds, and sheep coded-dark - the prior panel's own pricing for exactly what landed sums past 2.1, and it landed with zero verified fails and zero regressions across two page instances; I score 46.2, a +1.8 delta discounted below the gap-sum because redstone is distribution-without-logic (no inverter means no constructible gate), doors are unwired, the bow fires infinite arrows, and villages plus the in-world crafting table plus chunk eviction are now ten-round debts. The remaining climb through my domain is legible: ~2.0 redstone weights, 0.7 village weights, a third hostile archetype, and vehicles are the whole gap to the 50-anchor reading as conservatively over-satisfied rather than merely satisfied. Ties broken downward throughout; the survival loop is no longer aspirational text but the thin-vs-Minecraft depth of every family (two hostiles, ~25 recipes, four biomes, no enchanting/brewing analogs) is what holds the number in the mid-40s.
